articles.title: Why are non-farm payrolls being released on Good Friday? Here's what's open and what isn't
articles.expand: Tomorrow is one of those rare calendar collisions: the March non-farm payrolls report is released at 8:30 am ET on Good Friday — a day when the stock market is closed. Good Friday has been a NYSE holiday almost every year since 1864. It's the only stock market holiday that isn't also a federal holiday. The date moves around because it's tied to the...

articles.title: Crude Prices Surge as President Trump Vows to Escalate Iran War
articles.expand: May WTI crude oil (CLK26 ) today is up +10.20 (+10.19%), and May RBOB gasoline (RBK26 ) is up +0.1174 (+3.80%). Crude oil and gasoline prices are sharply higher today, with crude posting a 3.5-week high. Crude prices are rallying after President Trump pledged more aggressive action against Iran over...

articles.title: Pres Trump fires Attorney General Pam Bondi
articles.expand: Trump is reported to have fired Atty. Gen. Pam Bondi. Fox news reports that he is eyeing Lee Zeldin as a replacement, but Todd Blanche is reportedly the interim AG. Trump has reportedly been frustrated with Bondi's handling of the Jeffrey Epstein files, and Zeldin's name has come up most frequently in discussions of potential replacements, though n...

articles.title: Iran war jeopardizes global food prices
articles.expand: You think soaring Oil prices are the main problem? Are you worried about the impact of energy prices on inflation? Think again.

articles.title: WTI rallies as Trump signals continued military action against Iran
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) Crude Oil rebounds sharply on Thursday, rising more than 8% on the day, as ongoing tensions in the Middle East continue to keep a geopolitical risk premium embedded in prices amid supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: ECB's Villeroy: The next change in rates is highly likely to be upwards
articles.expand: Villeroy is set to retire but he's out with some fresh comments: It's far too early to predict a timetable for rate hikes but it's clear we have the capacity to act whenever it's necessary As of today, we are closer to the ECB's adverse scenario than the baseline The 'far too early' line excludes the upcoming meeting and that mostly aligns with the...

articles.title: NFP Preview: Can the labor market withstand the "Stagflation" Storm? Implications for the DXY & Dow Jones
articles.expand: NFP Preview: Can the labor market withstand the "Stagflation" storm? Geopolitical oil shocks are reigniting inflation, trapping the Fed between fighting rising energy prices and a cooling jobs market. Get consensus forecasts for the March employment report (NFP, wages, unemployment) and scenario-based reactions for the DXY and Dow Jones.

articles.title: Fed’s Logan: Payroll gains have been pretty weak
articles.expand: Lorie Logan, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of Dallas, said that the Fed should not let balance sheets distract them from their main mission. Logan also claimed that central bank balance sheet growth isn’t bad if it meets the public's need during a speech at her bank on Thursday.

articles.title: Fed's Logan: I wasn't convinced inflation was easing enough even before the war started
articles.expand: Comments from the Dallas Fed President: Was quite challenging to do most recent round of Fed forecasts Swift war resolution may mean economic impact might be pretty moderate Policy is positioned to respond to data, Fed prepared to make adjustments as needed US has some buffers to impacts from the war Key question is if war disruptions induce invest...

articles.title: Something of a turnaround: What does the bond market see?
articles.expand: There is a bounce in the war trade after the market open. It's a tough one because it's a long weekend and tomorrow's non-farm payrolls report is also a tricky one (though I don't think it matters that much). The big question is: Will Trump escalate or cut-and-run? For most of this week, the market was pricing in Trump declaring victory and walking...

articles.title: Eurozone: Energy shock risks and softer inflation dynamics – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas analysts assess how the new energy shock from war in Iran and higher Oil and gas prices compares with 2022 for the Eurozone. They argue the current backdrop is less inflationary, with weaker demand and fewer supply constraints, and central banks now more reactive.

articles.title: Euro area inflation rises on energy shock, core trends stay limited
articles.expand: Euro area inflation rose to 2.5% mainly due to higher energy prices driven by tensions in Iran, while core inflation remained subdued. Price pressures have not broadly spread across the economy, but second-round effects may emerge over time. The European Central Bank is likely to respond cautiously, with limited need for aggressive rate hikes.

articles.title: CHF: Small wins support firm currency – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley discusses recent Swiss inflation data and implications for the Swiss Franc. Foley notes that low Swiss CPI reduces pressure on the SNB to cut rates below zero, while credible FX intervention and safe-haven demand should keep CHF firm.

articles.title: BoC: Dovish patience with geopolitical risks – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities analysts characterize the Bank of Canada’s (BoC) March Summary of Deliberations as slightly dovish, consistent with the March 18 decision. They stress patience, citing inflation near target, weaker GDP, housing softness and labour market headwinds.

articles.title: How have interest rate expectations changed after this week's events?
articles.expand: Rate hikes by year-end ECB: 71 bps (62% probability of rate hike at the next meeting) RBA: 62 bps (71% probability of rate hike at the next meeting) RBNZ: 60 bps (94% probability of no change at the next meeting) BoE: 55 bps (65% probability of no change at the next meeting) BoJ: 53 bps (60% probability of rate hike at the next meeting) BoC: 44 bps...

articles.title: ECB's Simkus: Too early to say what we'll need to do in April
articles.expand: Too early to say what we'll need to do in April Caution needed on interest rates as situation is changing We see rising consumer inflation expectations, but I wouldn’t overplay that It's important to maintain credibility ECB has shown it’s an institution that can deal with an inflation shock with decisive action ECB's Simkus has emphasized a cautio...

articles.title: Swiss CPI Rises to 0.3% yoy, But Underlying Inflation Stays Soft
articles.expand: Swiss inflation remained subdued in March, with CPI rising 0.2% mom, below expectations of 0.5% mom. Annual inflation edged up from 0.1% yoy to 0.3% yoy, also missing forecasts of 0.5% yoy. The data highlights a continued lack of underlying price pressure despite some pickup in energy-related components. The breakdown shows a clear divergence. Impo...

articles.title: Switzerland March CPI +0.3% vs +0.5% y/y expected
articles.expand: Prior +0.1% Core CPI +0.4% y/y Prior +0.4% Even with the surge in energy prices, the impact on Swiss price pressures is less profound that what we saw with the Eurozone. Even the monthly estimate was +0.2%, missing on expectations of +0.5%. As such, the jump in headline annual inflation was also less than expected. This reaffirms that even with ene...

articles.title: South Korea's Lee urges prompt passage of $17 bln extra budget amid Middle East energy crisis
articles.expand: South Korean ​President Lee Jae Myung on ‌Thursday urged parliament to promptly pass a 26.2 trillion won ($17.3 ​billion) supplementary budget to ​shore up the economy amid "the ⁠worst energy security threat" ​posed by the Middle East ​crisis.

Data from Ghana Statistical Service (GSS) show the CPI rose to 264.8 in March 2026 from 256.5 [...] The post March inf...

articles.title: CBA currency exchange rates (02.04.2026)
articles.expand: According to the official exchange rates of the Central Bank of Azerbaijan for today, the US dollar remained unchanged at 1.7000 manats, the euro decreased by 0.2% to 1.9615 manats, while 100 Russian rubles rose by 0.9% to 2.1087 manats.

articles.title: As stock markets tank, this FTSE 100 share looks cheap to me!
articles.expand: The US-Iran war has caused stock markets to crash worldwide. This FTSE 100 stock has been hit hard, but I'd like to buy even more of these cheap shares. The post As stock markets tank, this FTSE 100 share looks cheap to me! appeared first on The Motley Fool UK .

articles.title: Canada Plugs Nation as ‘Reliable’ Energy Partner as Oil Soars
articles.expand: Canada’s top trade official plugged his nation as a stable and reliable energy supplier amid the latest jump in oil prices as President Donald Trump said military operations on Iran could escalate, potentially prolonging disruptions to oil flows through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Markets dip as Trump’s war speech fails to inspire - Market close
articles.expand: The S&P/NZX 50 Index closed at 12,902.15, up 76.27 points or 0.59%.

articles.title: Chart alert: Risk off after Trump’s speech - intraday outlook on S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, AUD/USD, Gold (XAU/USD), and WTI crude oil
articles.expand: Global markets have shifted back into risk-off mode after Trump’s hawkish stance on Iran dampened de-escalation hopes. Equities and AUD/USD weakened as bearish momentum resumed, while the US dollar strengthened. WTI crude oil surged above $102.25, supported by supply concerns, but gold’s recent rally shows signs of exhaustion. Technical signals sug...

articles.title: Australia faces ‘sharp recession’ warning as Middle East conflict drives oil prices
articles.expand: Economists predict Australia’s economy could suffer its worst quarterly fall since the 1990s outside of the Covid pandemic.

articles.title: How can the Fed reduce its balance sheet?
articles.expand: Since the Great Financial Crisis of 2008, the Federal Reserve has amassed roughly $6.6 trillion in assets, primarily in the form of Treasury securities and government-guaranteed mortgage-backed securities. Some, including President Trump’s nominee to be the next chair of the Federal Reserve Board, have suggested that the Fed should reduce the size ...

articles.expand: SYDNEY, April 2 Reuters Job vacancies in Australia rebounded to their highest in more than a year in the February quarter, data showed on Thursday, led by gains in the construction, retail and accommodation sectors. Figures from the Australian Bureau of Statistics ABS showed vacancies rose 2.7 in the three months to February, following a revised 0....

articles.title: Investors rush in hope that War resolution isn't an April Fool's – North American Mid-Week Market Update
articles.expand: Mid-Week update for North-American Markets – The narrative has changed for Markets, easing risk-sentiment for Stock Indexes and dragging both the US Dollar and Canadian Dollar. Taking a close look at NA index and currency performance combined with a USD/CAD intraday chart to spot what's next for American Markets.

The rally reached a high of 1.3966—just short of that next resistance band—before stalling and rotating back to the downside. By the close, the price had fallen back below the 1.3924–1.39...

articles.title: Euro comes out swinging: Can the "Trump Reversal" sustain EUR/USD's upside bias?
articles.expand: EUR/USD has surged towards 1.1600 due to "war optimism" following US President Donald Trump's comments and the hawkish stance of the ECB, which has created a yield differential favoring the Euro. The "Trump Reversal" seems to hold the key.

articles.title: BoE's Bailey: I still think markets are getting ahead of themselves by pricing rate hikes
articles.expand: If we have prolonged high energy prices and supply disruption, it will strain many countries quite seriously We will have to act if appropriate, but tackling the source of the energy price shock is most important I'm very clear we need to return inflation to target in a way that causes the least damage to growth and jobs We look at inflation expect...

articles.title: BOE Bailey: We will need to act if approriate.
articles.expand: Bank of England Governor Bailey struck a cautious tone, emphasizing risks around inflation, financial stability, and growth, while signaling the need to stay flexible on policy as conditions evolve. We will have to act if appropriate, but tackling source of energy price shock is most important If high energy prices and supply disruptions persist, i...

articles.title: Eurozone PMI Manufacturing Finalized at 45-Month High But War-Fueled Inflation Clouds Outlook
articles.expand: Eurozone PMI Manufacturing was finalized at 51.6 in March, up from 50.8, marking a 45-month high and signaling continued, albeit modest, expansion in the sector. However, according to Joe Hayes, Principal Economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, the Middle East war has already begun to leave a clear imprint on the sector. He noted that [&#8...

articles.title: BOJ policymaker Asada: How to deal with stagflation is a hard question for monetary policy
articles.expand: Rising oil prices put upwards pressure on inflation That also weighs on growth, creating a stagflationary trend How to deal with stagflation situation is a hard question for monetary policy In general, policymakers can deal with such a situation with a mix of fiscal and monetary policy But it is hard to control the economy with monetary policy alon...

articles.title: The Middle East Oil Shock Just Rewrote the ECB’s Interest Rate Outlook
articles.expand: It looked like the ECB was finally done with rate hikes, right up until the March inflation data started telling a different story.

articles.title: Is risk-on, back on? The AUDUSD is higher but it is not running quickly to the upside.
articles.expand: The AUDUSD typically trades as a classic risk-on/risk-off barometer. When equities are under pressure and yields are pushing higher, the pair tends to drift lower as traders reduce risk exposure. Conversely, when stocks firm and yields ease, AUDUSD often benefits from a more constructive, risk-on backdrop. Today, the pair is higher by about 0.62%, ...

The price did hold support near the high of the swing area - keeping the buyers in control. In the prior post, I wrote: A break above 0.8012 would open the door toward the next target near 0.804...

articles.title: Gold (XAU/USD) rallies 3%, eyes acceptance above the $4600/oz handle for bullish momentum to continue
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) is showing a technical recovery, breaking $4,600/oz as escalating Middle East tensions and a shifting correlation with rising Oil prices boost the "inflation hedge" narrative. Will the bullish momentum continue?

However, that momentum faded quickly, and buying interest dried up, leading to a reversal. The pair has since rotated back toward unchanged on the day, currently trading near 1.3180 after reaching a high of 1.3264...

articles.title: USDJPY moves further from the 160.00 level and cracks below the 200 hour MA target
articles.expand: The USDJPY has rotated lower after failing above the 160.00 level, a key psychological and technical barrier. Recall, the pair broke above that level on Friday, reaching a high of 160.455, but the breakout lacked follow-through. Yesterday, the price moved lower and tested an upward sloping trendline, where buyers stepped in and pushed the pair back...
